mnemonics: B.U.T.T. (Bytes, Utilization, Throughput, Transactions) - Think of the 'butt' of the server needing the right-sized capacity to handle its load.
keyPoints: Capacity is measured in specific units like CPU GHz, RAM GB, Storage GB/TB, and Network bandwidth Mbps/Gbps.
Utilization percentage (%) is a critical unit for measuring how much of a resource is currently in use.
Throughput (e.g., transactions per second, I/O operations per second) is a key performance unit, not just static size.
commonMistakes: Confusing storage capacity (GB) with data transfer capacity (Mbps/Gbps), leading to network bottlenecks.
Focusing only on maximum theoretical units (e.g., 1 Gbps NIC) instead of measuring actual achievable throughput.
Planning for peak capacity in only one unit (e.g., CPU) while ignoring others (e.g., RAM, I/O), creating an imbalanced system.
